# OMICRON

OMICRON is a protection, relay testing, diagnostics, and power-system validation specialist relevant to utility-interconnection, substation acceptance, and energization workflows for complex electrical infrastructure.

## Why it matters
- Adds a protection-and-testing layer that usually appears late, but often determines whether energization actually happens cleanly.
- Makes relay coordination, protection validation, and field acceptance more visible in the graph.
- Strengthens realism around utility-adjacent delivery rather than only packaged hardware presence.

## Best fit
- Sites with substation, relay, protection, or interconnection complexity.
- Programs where acceptance testing and protection validation are gating activities.
- Teams comparing not only equipment vendors but energization-critical specialists.

## Operational role in the graph
- Strengthens `power-and-electrical`, `epc-and-commissioning`, and `commissioning-and-operations`.
- Pulls `modular-substations` and `ats-and-transfer-systems` closer to actual energization and turnover.
- Makes protection testing a visible part of the site-delivery chain.
